visas.name: Engineer / Specialist in Humanities / International Services
visas.description: The most common work visa for white-collar professionals: software engineers, data scientists, designers, marketers, translators, and HR specialists. Requires a job offer from a Japanese company in a role matching your educational background.
eligibility: Bachelor's degree in a field relevant to the role (science or engineering for tech; humanities or business for non-technical positions)
10+ years of professional experience in the field can substitute for a degree in some cases
Job offer from a Japanese employer willing to sponsor the Certificate of Eligibility
Role must directly correspond to your educational or professional background
required_documents: Certificate of Eligibility (CoE) — applied for by the sponsoring employer
visas.processing_time: 1–3 months (CoE processed at Regional Immigration Services Bureau)
visas.renewal: Renewable indefinitely as long as you remain employed in a qualifying role
visas.path_to_pr: Eligible after 10 years continuous residence; reducible via Highly Skilled Professional points
visas.fees: CoE: free; Residence card update: ¥4,000; Re-entry permit: ¥3,000–¥6,000

visas.name: Highly Skilled Professional (HSP)
visas.description: Points-based visa for exceptional professionals. Requires 70+ points across academic background, career history, salary, age, and bonus categories (Japanese ability, Japanese university, etc.). Offers preferential treatment including the fastest path to permanent residency.
eligibility: Score 70 or more points on METI's Highly Skilled Professional points table
Three activity types: HSP-i (advanced academic research), HSP-ii (advanced technical/engineering), HSP-iii (advanced business management)
Job offer from a Japanese employer in most cases
Advanced degrees, high salary, and age under 35 significantly increase score
Score 80+ for a 1-year PR track; score 70–79 for a 3-year PR track
required_documents: Completed HSP points calculation table with supporting documentation
Salary certificate or employment contract showing compensation
Research publications, patents, or awards (if claiming bonus points)
CoE application or status change application
visas.duration: 5 years (HSP-1); indefinite activity permission (HSP-2 granted after 3 years as HSP-1)
visas.renewal: HSP-1 is renewable; HSP-2 is a permanent status change with no renewal needed
visas.path_to_pr: 1 year continuous residence (80+ points) or 3 years (70–79 points)

visas.name: Business Manager
visas.description: For foreign nationals who start or actively manage a business in Japan. Requires a registered Japanese company, a physical office, and either ¥5M paid-in capital or two full-time Japanese employees. The primary visa route for foreign entrepreneurs.
eligibility: Registered company in Japan (Kabushiki Kaisha or Godo Kaisha)
¥5M or more paid-in capital, OR two or more full-time Japanese resident employees
Dedicated physical office space — virtual addresses are generally not accepted
Applicant must hold an active management role, not be a passive investor
Viable business plan submitted with application
visas.renewal: Renewable while the business remains active and viable
visas.path_to_pr: 10 years continuous residence; can be accelerated via HSP business management category
visas.fees: CoE: free; KK incorporation: ¥150,000–¥250,000 in registration fees; GK: ¥60,000–¥100,000

visas.name: Specified Skilled Worker Type 1 (SSW-1)
visas.description: Created to address labor shortages in 12 designated sectors. Requires passing both a Japanese language test and a sector-specific skills examination. Limited to a 5-year cumulative stay with no family accompaniment in most cases.
eligibility: Pass the sector-specific skills test for one of 12 eligible industries
Japanese language proficiency: JLPT N4 or JFT-Basic A2 minimum
Age 18 or older

Eligible sectors: nursing care, building cleaning, materials processing, industrial machinery, electronics, construction, shipbuilding, automotive, aviation, lodging, agriculture, fisheries, food & beverage manufacturing
Holders of Type 2 Technical Intern Training certification are exempt from the skills test
required_documents: Japanese language test certificate (JLPT or JFT-Basic)
Employment contract with a registered Specified Skilled Worker organization
CoE application submitted by the employer
visas.duration: Up to 1 year per grant; 5-year maximum cumulative stay
visas.renewal: Renewable up to the 5-year total cap; family accompaniment not permitted except in exceptional circumstances
visas.path_to_pr: No direct path to PR; must transition to SSW-2 or an independent work visa
visas.fees: CoE: free; Skills examinations: ¥5,000–¥14,000 per exam

visas.name: Specified Skilled Worker Type 2 (SSW-2)
visas.description: The advanced tier of the Specified Skilled Worker program for workers with supervisory-level expertise. Currently available in construction and shipbuilding with expansion to additional sectors underway. Unlimited renewals and family accompaniment are permitted.
eligibility: Pass the SSW-2 level skills examination (higher standard than Type 1, includes supervisory assessment)
Currently available in: construction, shipbuilding and boat manufacturing
Additional sectors including food manufacturing expected to be added
required_documents: Employment contract with a qualifying employer
CoE application submitted by the employer
visas.duration: 3 years, 1 year, or 6 months per grant
visas.renewal: Unlimited renewals — no maximum cumulative stay cap
visas.path_to_pr: Eligible after 10 years continuous residence in Japan

visas.name: Digital Nomad Visa
visas.description: Introduced in March 2024. Allows remote workers employed by foreign companies to live in Japan for up to 6 months. Must earn ¥10M+ annually and hold private health insurance. Stays under 183 days are generally exempt from Japanese income tax.
eligibility: Annual income of ¥10M (approximately USD 65,000) or more from foreign sources
Employed by or running a business legally registered outside Japan
Comprehensive private health insurance that covers medical treatment in Japan
Citizen of one of the 49 countries with a visa exemption agreement with Japan (includes US, UK, most EU nations, Australia, Canada, South Korea)
required_documents: Proof of foreign employment or business registration
Income documentation: tax returns, payslips, or bank statements showing ¥10M+ annual income
Health insurance certificate valid in Japan
visas.processing_time: Apply at the Japanese consulate in your home country; typically 1–2 weeks processing time
visas.renewal: Cannot be extended or renewed; one 6-month stay per year
visas.path_to_pr: No path to PR through this visa; must depart and apply for a separate long-term status

visas.name: Startup Visa
visas.description: Allows foreign entrepreneurs to reside in Japan for up to 1 year to develop a business plan and incorporate a company. Administered through approved municipalities including Fukuoka City, Tokyo, and Osaka. Must be accepted into a local government startup support program before applying.
eligibility: Accepted into an accredited municipal startup support program
Credible business plan for a new venture intended to operate in Japan
Intent and ability to establish a company within the residence period
Available programs include Fukuoka City Startup Visa, Tokyo, Osaka, Kobe, and several other municipalities
required_documents: Acceptance letter from the qualifying municipal startup support program
Proof of sufficient personal funds to cover living expenses
visas.duration: 6 months to 1 year (business preparation period only)
visas.renewal: No renewal as Startup Visa; must transition to Business Manager visa before expiry
visas.path_to_pr: Via subsequent Business Manager visa; 10 years total continuous residence
visas.fees: Municipal program fees vary; visa application: ¥3,000–¥6,000

visas.name: Spouse or Child of Japanese National
visas.description: For spouses and biological or adopted children of Japanese citizens. Grants the most flexible working rights in Japan — no restrictions on industry, employer, or hours worked. Divorce or death of the Japanese spouse may affect visa status.
eligibility: Legal marriage to a Japanese national registered in Japan (spouse visa)
Biological or legally adopted child of a Japanese national (child of Japanese national visa)
Genuine marital relationship — immigration may interview both partners or request evidence of cohabitation
Marriage certificate and Japanese spouse's family register required
required_documents: Marriage certificate authenticated by Japanese authorities
Evidence of genuine relationship (photos, correspondence, joint financial documents)
Japanese spouse's income and tax documents
CoE application or status of residence change application
visas.processing_time: 2–4 months for CoE; faster for in-Japan status change
visas.renewal: Renewable while the marriage and relationship remain intact
visas.path_to_pr: 3 years continuous residence if married to and living with a Japanese national; or 1 year if the marriage has lasted 3+ years

visas.name: Student Visa
visas.description: For students enrolled in Japanese universities, graduate schools, language schools, or vocational colleges. Part-time work up to 28 hours per week is permitted with a separate work authorization. The standard entry route for those planning to transition to a work visa after graduation.
eligibility: Acceptance letter from an accredited Japanese educational institution
Sufficient financial means to cover tuition fees and living expenses for the duration of study
Qualifying institutions: universities, graduate schools, junior colleges, vocational schools, and Japanese language schools offering courses of 6 months or longer
required_documents: Certificate of Admission from the institution
Proof of financial support: bank statements or scholarship award letter
Certificate of Eligibility issued via the institution's Regional Immigration Services Bureau
visas.processing_time: 1–3 months; educational institutions typically manage the CoE application on behalf of students
visas.duration: Matches course length: typically 1–2 years for language school, up to 4 years for undergraduate programs
visas.renewal: Renewable to match course extensions or enrollment in a subsequent program
visas.path_to_pr: No direct path; must change status to a work or family visa after graduation before accumulating PR-qualifying residence
visas.fees: CoE: free; University tuition: ¥535,800–¥1,500,000/year; Japanese language school: ¥700,000–¥950,000/year

visas.name: Working Holiday
visas.description: A one-year cultural exchange visa for young adults from 30+ countries. Permits work to fund travel, though some bilateral agreements limit working for a single employer to 3 months. A popular low-commitment way to experience Japan before pursuing a longer-term visa.
eligibility: Citizen of a country with a Working Holiday agreement with Japan: Australia, Canada, UK, Ireland, New Zealand, Germany, France, South Korea, and 20+ others
Age 18–30 at time of application (up to 35 for citizens of Canada, Ireland, Denmark, and Norway)
No dependents accompanying you to Japan
Sufficient funds on arrival (typically ¥250,000 or equivalent)
One-time use only — cannot be repeated
required_documents: Completed Working Holiday visa application form
Evidence of return airfare or funds to purchase it
visas.processing_time: 1–4 weeks at the Japanese consulate in your home country
visas.renewal: Cannot be renewed or extended; one lifetime use per person
visas.path_to_pr: No path to PR via this visa; must depart and apply for a qualifying long-term status separately
visas.fees: Free for most nationalities; a small administrative fee applies in some countries
